tool_name: Compressed Air Receiver Volume Sizing — Peak Demand Buffer, Pressure Drop & ASME Certification

inputs.name: Compressor Free Air Delivery (C_fad)
inputs.note: FAD at reference conditions: ISO 1217 — 1 bar(a), 20°C, 0% RH

inputs.name: Minimum Acceptable System Pressure — Cut-In / Process Minimum (P_min)
Duration of Peak Demand Event (t_peak)
inputs.note: Pneumatic tool burst: 5–15 s; production line demand spike: 30–120 s

inputs.name: Safety Factor for ASME / PSSR Receiver Rating

formulas: Q_deficit_m3 = Math.max(0, peak_demand_m3min - compressor_capacity_m3min) * peak_duration_s / 60   // [m³ FAD] Net Demand Deficit During Peak (Q_deficit) Reference: Volume of air that must be supplied from storage: Q_def = (D_peak − C_fad) × t_peak

V_r_m3 = Q_deficit_m3 / ((P_max_bar - P_min_bar) / 1.013)   // [m³] Required Receiver Volume — Pressure Drop Method (V_r) Reference: ISO 1217 / CAGI — V_r = Q_FAD / [(P_max − P_min) / P_atm]; P_atm = 1.013 bar

V_rule_m3 = compressor_capacity_m3min / 7   // [m³] Recommended Receiver Volume — Rule-of-Thumb Check Reference: Industry rule of thumb: V_receiver ≈ C_fad (m³/min) / 7 for stable pressure band; CAGI handbook §3

V_design_m3 = Math.max(V_r_m3, V_rule_m3) * receiver_pressure_vessel_factor   // [m³] Design Receiver Volume (with safety factor) Reference: Final design volume: larger of peak-demand and rule-of-thumb, × safety factor

D_vessel_mm = Math.pow(V_design_m3 * 6 / Math.PI, 1/3) * 1000; P_design_bar = P_max_bar * 1.1; S_allow_mpa = 138; E_joint = 0.85; t_wall_mm = P_design_bar * 0.1 * D_vessel_mm / (2 * S_allow_mpa * E_joint - 1.2 * P_design_bar * 0.1)   // [mm] ASME / PSSR Minimum Wall Thickness (t_wall) for Cylindrical Shell Reference: ASME BPVC VIII Div.1 UG-27 — Barlow thin-wall; 10% design overpressure; SA-516-70 allowable S=138 MPa

T_on_min = V_design_m3 * (P_max_bar - P_min_bar) / (1.013 * compressor_capacity_m3min); T_cycle_min = V_design_m3 * (P_max_bar - P_min_bar) / (1.013 * (compressor_capacity_m3min - peak_demand_m3min / 2))   // [min] Compressor On/Off Cycle Time (T_cycle) Reference: T_on = V·ΔP/(P_atm·C_fad) — compressor cycle time; minimum 3 min on-time recommended to prevent motor overheating

V1.error_msg: P_min must be less than P_max — check pressure band inputs

V2.error_msg: Compressor on-time < 3 minutes: motor short-cycling risk — increase receiver volume or reduce pressure band

V3.error_msg: Receiver > 1 m³ above 10 bar: PSSR 2000 / PED 2014/68/EU category III vessel — notified body inspection and written scheme of examination mandatory

smart_warnings.message: Peak demand > 150% of compressor FAD: receiver will empty rapidly — evaluate additional compressor or demand-side management; verify peak event frequency
Pressure band < 1 bar: very small storage window — tight band requires frequent compressor cycling; widen band to minimum 1.5 bar or add receiver volume
Large high-pressure receiver: stored energy exceeds 250 bar·L — perform consequence analysis per API 521; locate receiver in blast-resistant area
Calculated wall thickness < 4 mm: minimum practical plate thickness for receiver fabrication is 4 mm per ASME BPVC; use 4 mm minimum
Compressor capacity meets peak demand with no deficit: receiver sized by rule-of-thumb only — primarily for pressure stability and wet air separation, not storage
